verdict: The AI displacement risk for Marine Biologists is moderate, with some tasks being automated but others requiring human expertise and judgment.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine Cognitive Tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks like analyzing characteristics of animals (task 9), conducting literature reviews (task 10), and performing administrative duties (task 7) are likely to be automated by AI systems.

reasoning_steps.title: Creative Judgment
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks that require creative judgment, such as informing and responding to the public regarding wildlife and conservation issues (task 3), are less likely to be automated by AI systems.

adjacent_careers.name: Wildlife Biologist
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Wildlife biologists and marine biologists often work together, and the skills required for one role can be applied to the other.

adjacent_careers.name: Conservation Scientist
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Conservation scientists and marine biologists share similar goals and may work on similar projects, but conservation science is a broader field that encompasses more than just marine biology.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered tool helps identify marine species
recent_developments.impact: This development may lead to more efficient identification of marine species, but it also raises concerns about the accuracy and reliability of AI-generated results.

recent_developments.headline: AlphaFold reshapes structural biology research
recent_developments.impact: Predicted protein structures are now a default starting point for experimental design.

quotes.quote: AlphaFold has predicted the structure of nearly every known protein. Tasks that took a PhD student years now take seconds. The bottleneck has moved to the experiments and the questions.
quotes.speaker: Demis Hassabis
quotes.title: CEO, Google DeepMind; Nobel laureate in Chemistry 2024

quotes.quote: AI is the most profound technology humanity is working on, more profound than fire or electricity. In science it is already changing what is knowable.
quotes.speaker: Sundar Pichai
quotes.title: CEO, Google / Alphabet

quotes.quote: I can imagine a world where AI agents run thousands of experiments in parallel, and the human scientist's role becomes one of asking the right question and interpreting the result.
quotes.speaker: Fei-Fei Li
quotes.title: Co-Director, Stanford Institute for Human-Centered AI
quotes.source: The Worlds I See / public lectures
